title: HTTP JSON Route Handlers for todos and activity feed (CA/DDD adapters)
description: Expose REST-style JSON endpoints (Next.js Route Handlers or equivalent) for todo CRUD/list and activity feed list (append only if MVP requires). Handlers are thin adapters: parse request, invoke application use cases, map errors to HTTP status. Remove or demote default runtime use of src/fixtures/activity-feed.json and ACTIVITY_FEED_FIXTURE_PATH; static fixture may remain for non-default test utilities only if documented. Deprecate or redirect legacy fixture route from 11-backend-activity-feed-fixture-route in favor of persisted feed API.
acceptance: Documented HTTP JSON API covers at minimum: list/create/update/delete (or equivalent) for todos and list for activity feed; response shapes stable for frontend consumption.
Route Handlers do not contain domain rules; they delegate to application services/use cases only.
Activity feed data for normal dev and production-style runs is served from PostgreSQL via use cases, not from activity-feed.json as the default source.
Legacy fixture env ACTIVITY_FEED_FIXTURE_PATH is not used on the default happy path, or is gated to test-only with explicit documentation.
Integration tests in isolated containers assert status codes, JSON contracts, and error mapping for representative success and failure cases.
OpenAPI or shared TypeScript types for the API are optional but if absent, README lists endpoint paths and example payloads.
